perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence, except that either party reserves the right to disclose such information to consultants and advisors without prior consent from the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information, unless the disclosing party deems it necessary for business purposes to disclose such information to third parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the confidential treatment of information. The original text states that Confidential Information shall not be disclosed to any third party without prior written consent of the other party, while the modified version says either party reserves the right to disclose such information to consultants and advisors without prior consent from the other party. The addition of the second half the sentences creates in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence, except that either party reserves the right to disclose such information to consultants and advisors without prior consent from the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information, unless the disclosing party deems it necessary for business purposes to disclose such information to third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidential information shall not be disclosed to any third party without prior written consent, creating a strict requirement. However, the changed text introduces exceptions by allowing disclosure to consultants and advisors without consent, and to third parties if deemed necessary for business purposes, which contradicts the initial strict confidentiality requirement. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The contradiction is that one part of the text asserts a strict confidentiality obligation, while another part permits specific disclosures without prior consent.

perturbation.original_text: If any party provides any of the other parties with written notification of its request for return of its Confidential Information, then the other party(ies) shall promptly return (unless destroyed pursuant to the immediately following sentence) to the notifying party all materials and information comprising the notifying party's Confidential Information, including any and all copies, facsimiles and reproductions thereof, and any other material containing or reflecting any materials or information in the Confidential Information. All other documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's representatives based on the materials or information in the Confidential Information shall be destroyed and such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the notifying party.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party provides any of the other parties with written notification of its request for return of its Confidential Information, then the other party(ies) shall make reasonable efforts to return the Confidential Information to the notifying party, and may retain copies for archival purposes. All other documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's representatives based on the materials or information in the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, at the discretion of the Receiving Party, and such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the notifying party, if requested.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates prompt return of Confidential Information and the destruction of derived documents. The modified text contradicts this by allowing retention of copies and discretionary destruction of derived documents. This undermines the confidentiality obligations and creates uncertainty regarding the extent of the Receiving Party's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any party provides any of the other parties with written notification of its request for return of its Confidential Information, then the other party(ies) shall make reasonable efforts to return the Confidential Information to the notifying party, and may retain copies for archival purposes. All other documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's representatives based on the materials or information in the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, at the discretion of the Receiving Party, and such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the notifying party, if requested.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by modifying the obligation to return Confidential Information. The original text mandates prompt return and destruction of derived documents. The changed text, as well as the contradicted text, allows the Receiving Party to retain copies and have discretionary destruction of derived documents, which directly conflicts with the stricter obligations in the original text by permitting actions that were previously prohibited. This is a direct conflict impacting the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Subject only to the Receiving Party's limited use of the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above in this Agreement, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, and the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party shall have a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes. Subject only to the Receiving Party's limited use of the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above in this Agreement, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, and the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The clause stating all intellectual property remains with the disclosing party contradicts the newly added exception granting the Receiving Party a perpetual license. This contradiction makes it unclear who owns the rights to derived intellectual property and under what conditions it can be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party shall have a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes. Subject only to the Receiving Party's limited use of the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above in this Agreement, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, and the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception granting the Receiving Party a perpetual, non-exclusive license. This directly contradicts the original statement that all right, title, and interest, including intellectual property, remains the sole property of the Disclosing Party. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text because the contradiction arises within the same section after the modification. The addition of the license creates a direct conflict over ownership and usage rights of the confidential information and intellectual property, hence a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one (1) year from the effective date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one (1) year from the effective date hereof, unless either party provides written notice of termination, in which case the agreement will terminate immediately.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the duration of the agreement. Originally, the agreement was fixed at one year. The change allows either party to terminate the agreement immediately with written notice, contradicting the one-year term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one (1) year from the effective date hereof, unless either party provides written notice of termination, in which case the agreement will terminate immediately.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed term of one year. The changed text introduces a termination clause that allows either party to terminate the agreement immediately with written notice. This directly contradicts the original one-year term, creating a structural flaw in the agreement as the term is no longer fixed due to the termination clause. The changed text and the contradicted text are the same but the modification introduced creates an in-text contradiction.
